author: dunkin-novice
goal: Rebuild the daily-mode result rendering on /reading.html?mode=daily to match the design doc's ScreenDailyAfter — a single big centered card with aura glow, bilingual orientation + Roman numeral eyebrow, bilingual card name + italic-serif archetype line under the Thai name, lucky-color chip, and a dedicated For-today interpretation panel with EN + TH bodies and a bilingual Keyword row. Reuses the data-spread-count='1' attribute and helpers (getMajorArcanaRoman, buildLuckyColorChip, .spread-archetype) already wired for question-mode Quick Pull, so the daily card carries the same ceremonial layout instead of the old plain-text caption stack.
done: js/reading.js renderDailyDetails: single-card branch (the 1-card daily result, distinct from full-mode Celtic Cross) rebuilt to emit data-spread-count='1' on readingContent + .panel--spread, .panel--question-recap eyebrow (Your card · today / ไพ่ของคุณ · วันนี้), .panel--spread[data-spread-count='1'] containing one .reading-spread-card with buildCardArt thumb + bilingual orientation+roman eyebrow + EN serif name + Thai serif name + .spread-archetype italic-serif row joining archetype_en · archetype_th + buildLuckyColorChip pulled from card.color_palette[0], then a new .daily-interp-panel with bilingual header (For today / สำหรับวันนี้), EN tarot_imply_en + TH tarot_imply_th paragraphs, a dashed divider, and a .daily-interp-panel__keyword-row showing bold 'Keyword' label + first keywords_light value + bilingual separator + bold 'คำสำคัญ' + same Thai value. Preserved all data injection points: createDailyDetails(card) wrap, appendReadingInternalLinks, persistReadingHistory, openCardSheet click binding on the card. css/reading.css §18 added: .spread-archetype italic-serif gold-deep line (reused by both Quick Pull and daily); .daily-interp-panel white-surface card with --mt-soft-shadow + header + bilingual __body--en/th paragraphs + dashed __divider + flex-row __keyword-row with bold __keyword labels and pale __keyword-sep middle-dot. No hex literals — all --mt-* tokens. reading.html static markup NOT touched — design-doc structure all JS-injected by renderDailyDetails.
verified: md5 canonical == www == ios for js/reading.js and css/reading.css
git diff --stat HEAD~1 HEAD shows 2 files changed, 224 insertions, 2 deletions — entirely within renderDailyDetails single-card branch (no impact to renderQuestion or renderFull)
cp + cap sync ios completed; no plugin errors
notes: PER USER DECISION (AskUserQuestion): keep archetype as a single italic-serif line under the Thai name joining EN and TH archetype with ' · ' separator (Option 1) instead of a stacked badge. PER USER DECISION: the .daily-interp-panel is a dedicated daily-only surface — Quick Pull's interpretation is rendered via the existing .spread-inline-text; daily gets its own panel because the design treats the For-today copy as a labeled block, not just a caption. Reuses helpers added in the prior phase-5 question-mode rebuild (getMajorArcanaRoman + buildLuckyColorChip, see commit c59d4a5) — DRY across Quick Pull and daily single-card layouts. The data-spread-count='1' CSS cascade from §5 of reading.css also catches the daily card, so aura glow + centered card art come for free. The amend (initial split commit had CSS only due to a Read-then-Edit ordering mistake) was folded into a single commit via `git commit --amend --no-edit` so the daily-result patch lands as one logical unit.
filed: The Phase 4 daily-result test data is light on cards where archetype_en differs meaningfully from archetype_th — a content audit should verify all 78 cards have both fields populated, otherwise the ' · ' separator will look unbalanced.

Keyword row currently shows only keywords_light[0] (first comma-separated value) — could grow to show top 2-3 keywords as a chip row in a future iteration, but the design doc shows only one keyword so this is faithful for now.

css/reading.css header comment in §1 still says '.panel--celtic-cross layout (Full reading) is intentionally NOT styled here' — that note is stale after the following celtic-cross commit; left intact pending a docs sweep.
affects: meowtarot.com/reading.html?mode=daily — daily reading result page now renders the ceremonial 1-card design layout (big centered card art with aura glow, bilingual orientation + Roman numeral if Major Arcana, EN+TH card name, italic-serif bilingual archetype line, lucky-color chip, and a dedicated For-today interpretation panel with EN + TH paragraphs and a bilingual Keyword row). Replaces the prior Phase 4 plain-text caption stack.

author: dunkin-novice
goal: Rebuild the Celtic Cross full-reading result on /reading.html?mode=full to match the design doc's ScreenCelticCross — gold number badges (1-10) on each card slot, a reordered right-column staff so the top-to-bottom Self/Environment/Hopes/Outcome sequence matches the design (the existing styles.css mapping had Outcome at the top), and a new Ten cards · ten doors summary panel beneath the cross+staff spread showing all 10 cards in a 2-column grid with number + italic-serif card name + bilingual position label. JS-only DOM emission + CSS additions; reading.html static markup untouched.
done: js/reading.js renderFull: positions.forEach now receives slotIdx (third arg) and prepends a JS-injected 1..10 to each .celtic-slot before buildCardArt, so each card carries a gold circle number in its top-left corner matching the design's slot numbering (1 Self, 2 Crossing, 3 Foundation, 4 Past, 5 Crown, 6 Future, 7 Self-view, 8 Environment, 9 Hopes-Fears, 10 Outcome). After spreadPanel.appendChild + readingContent.appendChild(spreadPanel), a new isCelticCross-gated block appends .panel--celtic-summary with a bilingual header (Ten cards · ten doors / สิบใบ · สิบประตู) and a 2-column .celtic-summary__grid containing 10 .celtic-summary__item rows, each a gold number badge + flex column with EN italic-serif card name + bilingual position label. All existing data injection points preserved: persistReadingHistory, trackRawCompletion, openCardSheet click on each slot, buildCardArt, full-interpretation-panel, full-integration-panel, takeawayPanel, retentionPanel, appendReadingInternalLinks. css/reading.css §17 added: .celtic-slot { position: relative } + .celtic-slot__badge gold circle (22px, plum-deep italic-serif number, 2px white border, absolute top-left -6px / -6px, z-index 3); hide .spread-label / .spread-orientation on celtic-cross slots (position + orientation info now lives in the summary panel below); right-column staff reorder via grid-area overrides so Self (slot 7, position=power) sits at row 1/4, Environment at 2/4, Hopes at 3/4, Outcome at 4/4 — matches design's top-to-bottom order which the existing styles.css mapping had reversed; .panel--celtic-summary white-surface card with --mt-soft-shadow + header flex-row (EN serif title + small TH subtitle) + 2-column .celtic-summary__grid + per-item flex layout (gold number + name + position text block); single-column fallback at @media max-width 380px.
verified: md5 canonical == www == ios for js/reading.js and css/reading.css

git diff --stat HEAD~1 HEAD shows 2 files changed, 227 insertions, 3 deletions — entirely within renderFull's positions.forEach + isCelticCross block + css/reading.css §17 (no impact to renderDailyDetails or renderQuestion)
cp + cap sync ios completed; no plugin errors
isCelticCross gate ensures non-celtic full readings (rare) bypass the summary panel and badge logic entirely — backward compatible
notes: PER USER DECISION: split this from the daily-result rebuild so reviewers can revert independently if the Celtic Cross layout has issues. The slot numbering (1-10) follows the standard Celtic Cross spread ordering documented in the existing positions array — the badges are purely visual reinforcement of order already encoded in the data. Right-column staff reorder is via grid-area, NOT by reordering DOM, so existing slot.id selectors and the openCardSheet click binding still resolve by index. Hiding .spread-label + .spread-orientation on celtic slots is a design-driven cleanup — the on-card UI per the design shows only number + art; orientation and position read out in the summary panel. The summary panel intentionally uses EN italic-serif for the card name (matches Quick Pull / Daily styling) but keeps the position label bilingual to honor en-th-parity. The .panel--celtic-summary single-column fallback at <=380px protects iPhone SE (375px) — the 2-column grid would be cramped at that width.
filed: Once the auto-log append for this commit lands, the Phase 5 reading.html surface (Quick Pull + Story + Daily + Celtic Cross) is structurally complete — all four result layouts now match the design doc. Next phase should be QA on real device sizes (iPhone SE 375, iPhone 14 390 — iPad probably out of scope since this is a mobile-first habit app).

The celtic-summary panel name text currently uses card.card_name_en (English) — could add a Thai-name sub-line under each item for full bilingual parity, but the design shows only one name per slot and adding a second would crowd the 2-col grid. Audit during the next QA pass.

Right-column grid-area override (.celtic-slot--power → 1/4 etc.) is keyed off the --power, --environment, --hopes, --outcome modifier classes — if a future redesign changes the modifier class names in renderFull, the CSS reorder will silently no-op. Worth adding a doc note in styles.css at the celtic-cross-grid definition.
affects: meowtarot.com/reading.html?mode=full — Celtic Cross result page now renders gold number badges (1-10) on each card slot, right-column staff reordered to Self/Environment/Hopes/Outcome (top-to-bottom), and a Ten cards · ten doors summary panel below the spread with all 10 cards in a 2-col grid (number + italic-serif name + bilingual position). The cross+staff spread layout itself is unchanged; this is a visual upgrade on top of the existing Phase 4 structure plus a new summary surface.
